2.name: Basic
2.description: If you do this because of your preference, you are being gaslit or need help. Nobody wanted to do this, and now the hand of fate chose you. Basic shit gets you nowhere, it takes your time, saves somebody else that time.
2.effect: Basic tasks don't fail, they just take longer.

3.name: Safe
3.description: If you do something the easy way, it usually means somebody else pays for it the hard way. Life is hard and if you're not chipping in, you are riding on.
3.effect: Safe tasks don't fail, you just take your sweet ass time.

4.name: Complex
4.description: Complex tasks bring nice payouts but can fail, which might require you to backtrack and reattempt. Nothing is guaranteed.

5.name: Professional
5.description: Professional shit is where the success is. There are some inevitable Pitfalls on your path, but a little bit of cunning can make a big difference.

6.name: Specialist
6.description: Specializations require a lot of dedication, so do the specialist tasks. Pitfalls are plenty, and being extra careful is the way.

7.name: Expert
7.description: You know solutions to most problems your job will try to surprise you with. Consequences of failing are often massive, so you need to plan ahead to avoid Pitfalls and the ways they can Backfire.

8.name: Elite
8.description: You have contributed to the current state of your industry. Actions of this magnitude Make Waves, so there will be people who will want to know.

9.name: Judge
9.description: Your opinion can decide what people think is possible. You have the infinity within your grasp and your word is absolute.
9.effect: This shit Makes Waves and can Backfire.

10.name: Top Dog
10.description: Those achievements create fans, good or bad. While not immediately life-changing, they will likely be vocal and/or influenced by your feats.
10.effect: You might find yourself with Friends in Unexpected Places.

11.name: Omega
11.description: Your actions reach top dogs in the area. You might find your future path blocked by some overenthusiastic Sigma maniac trying to deny his own made-up, but strongly correlating with truth, claims that you are just better.

12.name: Ascendant
12.description: Completing tasks of this magnitude might lead some desperate secret organization into recruiting you for a task of similar magnitude with preferably no chance of survival.
12.effect: Are you ready to die for some vague Echelon?

13.name: Architect
13.description: Your actions have reached the top of the pyramid, you have won the game. The Echelon would want you to stop chasing rabbits and do some Real Work, their idea of real work.
13.effect: Succeeding at this difficulty or higher will always spawn a new story.

14.name: Echelon
14.description: The Echelon hopes that you are not wasting your potential on your own egotistical ideas, and whatever you just did now is meant to Serve the Echelon.
14.effect: It may be jealous or feel threatened if not.

15.name: Zenith
15.description: Everybody's pretty close on top of the pyramid. Apex Success might entangle you into personal Ambitions of someone very powerful.

16.name: Paragon
16.description: It's no longer about ideologies of the groups, it's about the ideals of the individual.
16.effect: Apex Success will likely entangle you into personal Ambitions of someone very powerful.

17.name: Apex
17.description: This will get personal with someone and you will have to Leverage it out beforehand or afterwards.

18.name: Sovereign
18.description: No longer about individuals so much, it will create Fallout with somebody extra powerful.

19.name: Dominion
19.description: Actions of this magnitude will usually shake some status quo triggering a Crisis.

20.name: Overlord
20.description: Most people who reach this place do so only once in their lifetimes, only to be cursed into lifelong deprivation of such success. But that’s later, for now your action brings Tranquility.

21.name: Rift
21.description: Whatever lies ahead forgot the warm embrace of the human body and already has many aspects of your life in its reach and might require anonymous assistance.

22.name: Envoy
22.description: Your skill brings hope to the hopeless. Something beyond normal understanding requires you to take action that might lead to a Calamity.

success_failure_mechanics.success: Rolling equal to difficulty level is a success.
success_failure_mechanics.failure: Rolling a 1 shouldn't be a success, unless the GM wants it.

degrees_of_failure.basic_safe_tasks: Don't fail, they just take extra time in case of failure.
degrees_of_failure.backfire: Usually means punishment from the GM.
degrees_of_failure.pitfalls: Can prevent success and should be played by the GM to that aim.
degrees_of_failure.making_waves: Can place you next to unlikely allies or surprising enemies.
degrees_of_failure.friends_in_unexpected_places: Some of them good, some of them not even friendly.
degrees_of_failure.echelon_interest: Echelon may or may not be interested in your feats.
degrees_of_failure.real_work: Starts when Echelon is interested. It’s a curse.
degrees_of_failure.sigma_rival: A rival, rarely a fair player, often bundled with their own goons.
degrees_of_failure.serve_the_echelon: Serve the Echelon or accept the consequences.
degrees_of_failure.leverage: Work it or make someone feel Betrayed.
degrees_of_failure.crisis: When an Echelon wants you to stop.
degrees_of_failure.calamity: Creates a vacuum of power afterwards. Some things will pass, new will be born.
degrees_of_failure.asspull: It doesn't even have to make sense anymore.

resolution_mechanics.core_rule: Players roll a dice pool and add relevant modifiers based on collected upgrades so far.

rolling_system.rule: Number of sides you roll must be double your roll value, which relies on your skill value.
rolling_system.success_check: The result is compared against a target difficulty or opposed roll.

types_of_checks.static_checks: The player must meet or exceed a set difficulty threshold.
types_of_checks.contested_rolls: Two parties roll against each other, with the higher result winning the contest.
types_of_checks.degree_of_success: You can succeed at a higher difficulty level even if the challenge was lower, granting additional contextual benefits.
